notes: Raum is a reverb plugin (VST3 audio effect) with three distinct modes: Room (natural small-to-medium room simulation), Hall (large concert hall and epic space), and Grounded (a distinctive earthy, organic reverb character designed by NI). It is not a Kontakt instrument — it is a dedicated audio effects processor accessed via the VST parameter API using get_device_parameters and set_device_parameter. The CC mappings listed here follow convention for parameters where Raum supports external MIDI control or automation. Dry/Wet and Decay are the two primary parameters in any reverb workflow: Dry/Wet (CC 91) controls how much reverb is in the mix, while Decay (CC 23) sets the tail length. Size (CC 22) changes the perceived room or hall dimensions. Pre-Delay (CC 24) is a critical mixing tool — adding pre-delay separates the dry signal from the reverb onset for clarity in dense mixes. The Shimmer parameter (CC 25) adds a pitched, octave-up shimmer effect to the reverb tail for ethereal, pad-like reverb character. The Freeze function (CC 26) holds the current reverb tail indefinitely for drone and textural use.

Volume.description: Output level of the reverb plugin

Pan.description: Stereo pan of the wet signal

Expression.description: Overall wet level — dynamic control of the reverb presence in the mix

Modulation.description: Modulation depth — adds subtle pitch movement to the reverb tail for a more organic, less static character

Dry/Wet.description: Reverb wet/dry mix — primary blend control between dry signal and reverberated signal

Size.description: Room/Hall Size — perceived dimensions of the space from small room to enormous hall

Decay.description: Decay time — length of the reverb tail from short room to infinite space

Pre-Delay.description: Pre-delay — time before reverb onset begins; increases clarity in dense mixes by separating source from reverb

High-Cut.description: High-cut filter on the reverb tail — reduces high-frequency content for a warmer, darker reverb character

Low-Cut.description: Low-cut filter on the reverb tail — reduces low-frequency buildup for clarity in the low end

Shimmer.description: Shimmer — adds a pitched, octave-up shimmer effect to the reverb tail for ethereal, ambient character

Character/Freeze.description: Character control or Freeze toggle — in Freeze mode (CC > 64) holds the reverb tail indefinitely for drone textures
